data.title: Clifford Modern Living Deepens Precious Metal Bets in Major Transaction
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Clifford Modern Living Holdings Ltd ( (HK:3686) ) has provided an update.

Clifford Modern Living Holdings has placed an order using internal resources to purchase about 70,600 ounces of allocated silver bullion for roughly HK$42.9 million, marking its third silver investment as part of a broader precious metals strategy involving multiple silver and gold positions executed through a bank. While the third silver purchase alone is classified as a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, the aggregation of all recent silver and gold investments crosses the 25% threshold, making it a major transaction that requires reporting, public announcement and shareholder approval, which the company is able to obtain via written consent since no shareholder is deemed to have a material interest or must abstain from voting.

The move underscores the group’s ongoing use of precious metals as a treasury management tool, signaling a more substantial balance sheet exposure to bullion that could affect its risk profile and earnings volatility depending on metal price movements, while also highlighting the regulatory scrutiny and governance procedures tied to large-scale financial investments by Hong Kong–listed firms. For existing shareholders, the announcement clarifies that the enlarged investment portfolio in silver and gold is being executed within listing rule parameters without diluting voting rights or requiring a physical general meeting, potentially streamlining execution but concentrating more corporate resources in non-core financial assets.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3686) stock is a Buy with a HK$0.90 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Clifford Modern Living Holdings Ltd stock, see the HK:3686 Stock Forecast page.

More about Clifford Modern Living Holdings Ltd

Clifford Modern Living Holdings Ltd is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, which engages in treasury management activities that include investing in precious metals such as allocated silver bullion and both allocated and unallocated gold through banking channels to manage its financial resources and enhance returns.

The latest announcement is out from Nanjing Panda Electronics Company ( (HK:0553) ).

Nanjing Panda Electronics Company Limited announced the resignation of non-executive director Liu Jianfeng from the eleventh session of its board and all related board committee roles, citing work reassignment and adjustment as the reason. The company emphasized that his departure, effective upon submission of his resignation, will not reduce the board below its minimum quorum nor disrupt standardized operations or normal business activities.

Liu has completed all required handover procedures, and the company plans to elect a new director in accordance with statutory procedures as soon as possible, while publicly thanking him for his diligence and contributions during his tenure. He confirmed he has no disagreement with the board or management and that there are no other matters related to his resignation that need to be brought to shareholders’ attention, signaling a routine governance transition rather than a sign of internal conflict.

More about Nanjing Panda Electronics Company

Nanjing Panda Electronics Company Limited is a Chinese electronics company listed in Hong Kong, operating under a board structure that includes executive, non-executive, independent non-executive and employee directors. The company is governed by the PRC Company Law and its Articles of Association, reflecting a standard corporate governance framework for a mainland-listed issuer active in the electronics sector.

Alco Holdings ( (HK:0328) ) has shared an update.

Alco Holdings has announced the current composition of its board of directors, led by non-executive chairman Pan Ying and executive director Zheng Yuxing, alongside three independent non-executive directors, Chu Hoi Kan, Lam Chi Wing, and Tang Sher Kin. The company also detailed the membership and leadership of its audit, remuneration, and nomination committees, underscoring its adherence to corporate governance practices and the separation of oversight, pay, and nomination responsibilities among independent directors.

Independent director Chu Hoi Kan chairs the audit committee, with Lam Chi Wing and Tang Sher Kin serving as members, while Tang leads the remuneration committee and Lam chairs the nomination committee. This allocation of roles, with key committees headed by independent directors, is likely intended to enhance board oversight, strengthen investor confidence, and align the company with regulatory and market expectations for governance standards in Hong Kong.

data.title: Pico Far East Sets Terms and Timetable for Special Scrip Dividend
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Pico Far East Holdings ( (HK:0752) ) has provided an announcement.

Pico Far East Holdings has confirmed the scrip dividend arrangement tied to its special dividend of HK4.5 cents per share for the year ended October 31, 2025, giving shareholders the option to receive the payout in cash or in new fully paid shares. The issue price for these scrip shares has been set at HK$2.522, based on the average closing price over five trading days to April 10, 2026, and fractional share entitlements will be rounded down with any residual benefit accruing to the company.

The company will seek listing and permission to deal in the new scrip shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, with a circular and election form to be dispatched to shareholders on April 21, 2026 and election due by May 6, 2026. Share certificates for the scrip shares are expected to be mailed on May 26, 2026, with trading in these shares slated to commence on May 27, 2026, a timetable that underlines the company’s use of equity-based distributions to manage capital while maintaining shareholder flexibility.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0752) stock is a Buy with a HK$3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Pico Far East Holdings stock, see the HK:0752 Stock Forecast page.

data.description:

MECOM Power & Construction Ltd. ( (HK:1183) ) has provided an announcement.

MECOM Power and Construction Limited announced that Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu will retire as its auditor after the upcoming annual general meeting, following an inability to agree on audit fees for the 2026 financial year amid an anticipated expansion in audit scope. The board and audit committee stressed there were no disagreements or unresolved matters with Deloitte, and they do not expect the retirement to materially affect the timing or quality of the group’s 2026 annual audit and results.

The company plans to appoint OOP CPA & Co. as its new auditor, subject to shareholder approval at the AGM, to fill the vacancy left by Deloitte’s retirement. MECOM’s audit committee cited OOP’s experience with Hong Kong-listed companies, independence, resources and an audit fee aligned with the group’s scale, arguing the change will maintain audit quality and serve the interests of both the company and its shareholders.

More about MECOM Power & Construction Ltd.

MECOM Power and Construction Limited is a Hong Kong-listed engineering and construction group operating through subsidiaries, with a focus on power-related and infrastructure projects. The company serves markets requiring large-scale construction and technical services, and its expanding business is driving a broader audit scope and more complex financial reporting needs.

data.title: Sino-Ocean Subsidiary Flags RMB17.19 Billion in Overdue Debt and New Lawsuit
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sino-Ocean Group Holding ( (HK:3377) ) has shared an announcement.

Sino-Ocean Group Holding has disclosed new financial stress at its wholly owned subsidiary Beijing Sino-Ocean Group Holding, which reported overdue borrowing principal of RMB17.19 billion as of 31 March 2026. The overdue amount increased by RMB0.41 billion in March, mainly due to higher overdue bank loans, while public market financing remained unchanged and some entrusted and non-bank borrowings declined slightly.

The subsidiary also faced a new major litigation in March involving borrowing disputes of about RMB1.87 billion, adding to existing court cases. The group acknowledged that the mounting arrears and lawsuits are exerting adverse pressure on the subsidiary’s operations and debt-servicing capacity, but said day-to-day management and business remain normal as it negotiates with creditors and institutions to pursue holistic debt solutions and pledges ongoing regulatory disclosures.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3377) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.10 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sino-Ocean Group Holding stock, see the HK:3377 Stock Forecast page.

More about Sino-Ocean Group Holding

Sino-Ocean Group Holding Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated company engaged through its subsidiaries in property-related operations in mainland China. Its wholly owned unit, Beijing Sino-Ocean Group Holding Limited, is a key onshore financing and operating platform within the group, with activities funded by bank loans, public market financing, entrusted loans and borrowings from non-bank institutions.

An update from Nexteer Automotive Group ( (HK:1316) ) is now available.

Nexteer Automotive Group has adopted a Value Creation Plan (VCP) and approved a 2026 Grant that provides cash-based awards to senior management tied to performance and service conditions. The plan is structured without issuing new shares or using existing share capital, meaning it falls outside Hong Kong Listing Rules Chapter 17 and avoids any dilution for current shareholders.

Under the 2026 Grant, the board has awarded a total of 5,015,240 Performance Units and Restricted Units, representing a cash value of about USD4 million, to 15 senior managers, including the CEO and another executive director. Vesting depends on both tenure and the company achieving a three-year average return on invested capital of at least 4.5% over the 2026–2028 period, aligning management incentives with capital efficiency and long-term value creation.

data.title: Distinct Healthcare Moves Shareholder Communications Online Under New HKEX Rules
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Distinct Healthcare Holdings Limited ( (HK:2677) ) has provided an update.

Distinct Healthcare Holdings Limited has shifted to electronic dissemination of its corporate communications in line with amended Hong Kong listing rules effective 31 December 2023. Future documents such as annual and interim reports, meeting notices, listing documents and circulars will be published on the company’s website and the HKEX news platform in both English and Chinese, replacing routine distribution of printed copies.

The company will no longer send separate notifications when new documents are posted online, instead urging non-registered shareholders to monitor the websites proactively. Investors who still prefer hard copies can submit a request form or written request, with such elections remaining valid only until the end of the following financial year, signaling a clear push toward digital communications and reduced paper-based investor materials.

More about Distinct Healthcare Holdings Limited

Distinct Healthcare Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 2677. The group operates in the healthcare sector, providing medical and related clinical services, with its investor relations and regulatory disclosures focused on shareholders in the Hong Kong capital market.
